name: Dask Jobqueue On Slurm
summary: Launch Dask workers through Slurm with dask-jobqueue and bounded dry-run defaults.
description: This skill helps users configure Dask Jobqueue for Slurm-backed Python workloads while keeping worker resources, queue settings, logs, temporary directories, and scale limits explicit.

inputs.name: account
inputs.description: Site-specific Slurm allocation or account for Dask worker jobs.

inputs.name: queue
inputs.description: Slurm partition or queue for Dask worker jobs.

inputs.name: worker_shape
inputs.description: Cores, processes, memory, walltime, and job count for each Dask worker job.

inputs.name: python_environment
inputs.description: Optional virtualenv or Conda/Mamba environment containing dask, distributed, and dask-jobqueue.

outputs.name: worker_job_script
outputs.description: Generated Slurm worker job script from Dask Jobqueue.

outputs.name: scheduler_address
outputs.description: Dask scheduler address printed when RUN_DASK=1.

outputs.name: dask_result
outputs.description: Small JSON result report from the demonstration workload.

outputs.name: worker_logs
outputs.description: Slurm and Dask worker logs written under the configured log directory.

examples.title: Dry-run or launch a Dask Jobqueue Slurm demo

tests.description: Validate the manifest and referenced examples.
Run the wrapper with RUN_DASK=0 to inspect the generated worker job script, then set RUN_DASK=1 with a small job count on a test partition and confirm the result JSON is written.
